findings.title: Raw exception logging can leak URL credentials and response contents
findings.problem: The new {exc} logging expands previously safe summaries into str(exc). For httpx.HTTPStatusError raised by raise_for_status(), that string includes the full request URL; repository validation only blocks apikey= and explicitly allows other query params, so proxy tokens or userinfo can be persisted in logs. For pydantic.ValidationError, the string includes input_value, which can include malformed upstream response bodies. This violates the existing sanitization pattern in triggarr/search/engine.py that reduces HTTP errors to status/type and validation errors to counts.
findings.why_it_matters: Turns bounded diagnostics into raw exception logging on a network trust boundary: URL query tokens or userinfo and upstream response-body fragments can be persisted into logs that the sanitize-at-source pattern previously kept structurally clean.
findings.fix_hint: Do not log raw exc here. Keep HTTP {exc.response.status_code} for status errors and validation error ({exc.error_count()} issues) for validation failures, or move the existing sanitizer into a shared non-cyclic utility and use it from both call sites.

findings.title: Raw ValidationError logging bypasses the established _sanitize_exc convention
findings.problem: Same deviation for the pydantic.ValidationError branch: the diff replaces the structured count=exc.error_count() field with the raw {exc}/exc=exc. The established solution _sanitize_exc() handles this exact type explicitly - pydantic.ValidationError maps to 'validation error ({count} issues)' (engine.py line 42-43), which is essentially the pre-diff message this branch already produced. _on_job_error (scheduler.py line 408) and the CR-01 tracking handler both route pydantic.ValidationError through _sanitize_exc precisely because raw pydantic error output can echo back input field values. The established pattern is to summarize by error count, not dump the full validation-error repr.
findings.why_it_matters: A pydantic ValidationError's str() renders every failing field with its offending input value and location. If an *arr status response ever echoes a credential-bearing field, the raw validation-error text carries it into the log message; the diff abandons the codebase's summarize-by-count convention that made the leak structurally impossible at source.
findings.fix_hint: Route this branch through _sanitize_exc (triggarr/search/engine.py) or keep the summarize-by-count form: 'validation error ({exc.error_count()} issues)'.
